content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 22, 2017

Andrew Morris
Town Attorney
Town of Mammoth Lakes
P.O. Box 1609
Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. W-17-142

Dear Mr. Morris:

        This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Greg Eckert, a member of
the Town of Mammoth Lakes' Planning and Economic Development Commission, regarding the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You recently notified the
Commission that the government decision in question occurred on June 14, 2017 without Mr.
Eckert's participation, and the requested advice is no longer needed. Accordingly, we
acknowledge that you withdraw your request for advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                                             Sincerely,

                                                                             Jack C. Woodside
                                                                             General Counsel

                                                                    By:    L. Karen Harrison
                                                                           Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl


¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges withdrawal of the advice request because the government decision occurred on June 14, 2017 without Mr. Eckert's participation, making the requested advice no longer necessary.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no formal Q&A sections. Question and conclusion synthesized from letter body text. File No. W-17-142 indicates withdrawal status.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges withdrawal of the advice request because the government decision occurred on June 14, 2017 without Mr. Eckert's participation, making the requested advice no longer necessary.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter from the FPPC regarding an advice request from the Town Attorney of Mammoth Lakes concerning a potential conflict of interest issue. The request was withdrawn after the underlying government decision was made without the subject individual's participation.
